summary: Social ads should communicate the hook and proof while muted, using native framing without copying a viral source.
principles: Put the first promise in readable visual form — text overlay, product action, or visual contrast that communicates without audio.
Use captions, overlays, or product action to make the ad legible on silent autoplay — if it doesn't work muted, it doesn't work on mobile.
Adopt platform-native grammar (text overlays on TikTok, swipe-up on Reels) while preserving brand-specific design choices.
The first 3 seconds must be fully comprehensible without sound — the viewer decides to stay or scroll before audio even starts.
Dynamic captions that emphasize key words create visual rhythm that substitutes for audio emphasis on muted playback.
Platform-native doesn't mean platform-identical — the ad should feel like it belongs on the platform while being unmistakably the brand.
Test the ad's mute-friendliness by watching it with sound off before approval — if the story isn't clear, the overlay strategy needs rework.
apply_when: The primary placement is TikTok, Reels, Shorts, Instagram, or mobile feed.
The audience may watch without sound.
The ad uses narration but still needs instant comprehension.
avoid_when: The final placement is TV, cinema, or a sound-on landing page.
Native overlays would violate the brand's design system.
The required legal copy cannot be made readable in the chosen format.
failure_patterns: Ad that relies entirely on narration for comprehension: muted, the viewer sees pretty footage with no idea what's being sold.
Platform-native grammar applied without brand integration: the ad looks like a creator post but doesn't feel like the brand.
Text overlays that are too small, too fast, or too cluttered for mobile screens — if the viewer can't read the text while scrolling, it fails.
Captions that are verbatim narration transcripts without visual hierarchy — a wall of identical text isn't communication, it's subtitles.

cross_domain_notes.note: The hook must be visually self-contained — the contrast or question must read without audio since 85%+ of mobile viewers start muted.
Text overlay contrast against the background must exceed WCAG AA standards on mobile — thin white text on light backgrounds is invisible.
Center-compose key text and product moments for mobile's vertical center of attention; rule-of-thirds text gets lost on small screens.

execution_techniques: Write the ad's core message as a single text overlay that could appear in the first 2 seconds — if it can't be said in 5 words, it's too complex.
Use bold, high-contrast dynamic captions that scale up on emphasis words — this creates visual rhythm that replaces audio emphasis.
Place all critical text in the center 60% of the frame to account for platform UI overlays (username, description, buttons) that cover the edges.
A/B test the first frame with and without text overlay — if the no-text version doesn't communicate the promise, the visual strategy needs strengthening.
